blocks.content: The Ten Kingdoms of the Archipelago.
Among the ten kingdoms of 'Cartesia,' Dionas was an island located at the far eastern end.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The earthquake caused by Gamagin occurred in the fourth island, the Kingdom of Mitoa. Thanks to the other islands acting as breakwaters, Dionas suffered the least damage from the tsunami.
The captain, listening to Shirone's explanation, bit his pipe.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hmm, will it be alright? I know it's not the time to consider circumstances for flood victims, but the rumors are quite bad.
Although tied to 'Cartesia,' the Kingdom of Dionas was originally a place of exile for criminals.
The sea dragon Kaos spoke.

blocks.speaker: Kaos
blocks.content: The dynasty was established 120 years ago by pirates. And even now, the main source of national income is maritime plunder, making it a rather unsavory society.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It can't be helped. Until we eradicate the demon world, people need a safe place to stay.
Shirone left the captain's cabin.
Looking back from the top of the large ship, a spectacular view of numerous ships lined up unfolded.

blocks.speaker: Poine
blocks.content: Can we say it's smooth sailing so far? Of course, incidents and accidents happen every day…
From food fights to trivial disputes over sleeping areas, there was never a day without commotion.

blocks.speaker: Poine
blocks.content: Shouldn't we strengthen law enforcement? Reducing daily meals by half won't completely control people's emotions.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If we increase pressure too much, people might lose their composure. It's better to think of minor commotions as lubrication.

blocks.speaker: Poine
blocks.content: Well, that's true…
Poine looked at a sailing ship flying Arachne's flag on the left.

blocks.speaker: Poine
blocks.content: With that flag flying around, it's natural for people to feel deprived, isn't it?
It was a ship carrying Arachne's minister, and all the workers were armed soldiers.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It can't be helped. Arachne represents Cartesia. If they help, it will be much easier for flood victims to settle in Dionas.

blocks.speaker: Poine
blocks.content: Of course, there was another reason behind it.

blocks.speaker: Poine
blocks.content: They didn't volunteer to lead just for that reason. Their goal is probably to prepare for the upcoming Holy War reorganization. They want to lay the groundwork before fighting against world powers.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Leave the flag alone. There's no need to escalate things unless they claim extraterritoriality.
Poine's eyes narrowed.

blocks.speaker: Poine
blocks.content: Haha, with Messiah here, no one would dare do something so foolish. However, some fleet captains have raised objections about refusing an inventory check of warehouse goods. They suspect food is being siphoned off.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That might be true, but there must be another reason for not opening the warehouse.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: We have enough food. Make a conditional agreement that we'll conduct an inventory check if supplies run low.

blocks.speaker: Poine
blocks.content: Understood.
As Poine bowed and stepped back, Kaos pointed below deck.
Messiah, over there…
A small fast boat was quickly approaching from Arachne's sailing ship.
Confirming the messenger flag, Shirone took his apostles and jumped straight down to the first deck.
Knights in armor ignored ladders and leaped through the air one by one.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What's going on?
Passengers who were not friendly towards Arachne's sailing ship frowned at that moment.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Oh my.
A woman who climbed up using a ladder drew everyone's attention.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Who is she?
She was a beauty and a famous person recognizable even to non-Arachne citizens.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Barho Langi?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Miss Arachne.
Even Shirone, who had met her at an international beauty pageant, stared blankly at her.

blocks.speaker: Langi
blocks.content: Hello. Nice to meet you.
She greeted people with familiar eye contact as she crossed the deck with her escorts.

blocks.speaker: Langi
blocks.content: Shirone!
Langi's face lit up with joy.

blocks.speaker: Langi
blocks.content: You're really on this ship? When I heard rumors, I couldn't believe it. Oh, what a coincidence.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How did you get here?
There was no reason for Miss Arachne to accompany a refugee support mission.

blocks.speaker: Langi
blocks.content: Well…
Langi hesitated for a moment before sticking out her tongue.

blocks.speaker: Langi
blocks.content: I volunteered because I knew you were on this ship. There might not be another chance in the future.
Her last words carried significant meaning.
In the capital of Thormia, some of the demonic hordes heading south settled down.
They were the 7th Brigade of the 8th Legion.

blocks.speaker: Gustav
blocks.content: Here's today's spoils.
What they brought back from raiding villages was mostly neither food nor treasure but humans themselves.
Men, women, and children alike were dragged in line and became playthings for the demons before being consumed.
The horrific screams that echoed every night had even driven the animals away from the sinister mountains.

blocks.speaker: Gustav
blocks.content: …Now we can finally live a bit.
Gustav the Fourth was imprisoned in a special bone jail set up there.
Though almost healed from the fatal wounds he received at the 'Flower Garden,' the problem was his mental shock.

blocks.speaker: Zetaro
blocks.content: Harvitz... Harvitz…
Zetaro muttered with a vacant expression.

blocks.speaker: Zetaro
blocks.content: He betrayed us. Why? Why?
Balkan had told him everything about the incident, but he still couldn't believe it.

blocks.speaker: Smodo
blocks.content: Stop it already. How many days has it been? And is Harvitz your husband? What betrayal…
Picking at maggots, he cracked one with his front teeth and swallowed it before continuing.

blocks.speaker: Smodo
blocks.content: We just need to live doing what we want, right? Seems like he found something more interesting.
That was exactly why Zetaro was irritated.

blocks.speaker: Zetaro
blocks.content: I'm the best entertainer there is. Do you know how many humans I've killed? What could possibly be more interesting?

blocks.speaker: Smodo
blocks.content: I don't know. Maybe he discovered some new form of torture? Something beyond our imagination.
It wasn't entirely wrong.
Balkan, listening in from a corner of the bone jail, assessed the situation outside.

blocks.speaker: Balkan
blocks.content: My injuries are almost healed. It's not entirely impossible to escape, but…
What else did they have left?

blocks.speaker: Balkan
blocks.content: It's typical for us to kill plenty and enjoy plenty. Seems like Harvitz left because he got bored too.
Footsteps were heard.

blocks.speaker: Demons
blocks.content: Hey! Time to eat!
Having chewed on nothing but maggots all day, his stomach churned just hearing the sound.

blocks.speaker: Demons
blocks.content: Let's eat. What's on the menu today?
Demons over three meters tall approached, leading Natasha, who was chained by the neck.

blocks.speaker: Demons
blocks.content: Heh, guess what it is?
The food pushed through the bars looked like pig's feet and smelled rancid.

blocks.speaker: Natasha
blocks.content: How's your wound?

blocks.speaker: Demons
blocks.content: Much better. And you?
Scars and chain marks, playfully etched on Natasha's skin covered by a ragged cloak, were visible.

blocks.speaker: Natasha
blocks.content: It's fine. Eat up. It's not human flesh.
The demon yanked the chain.

blocks.speaker: Demons
blocks.content: Hey, hey! Stop with the unnecessary talk! Want to die?
Twelve blades sprung from the device around Natasha's neck, piercing her skin.
Blood trickled down her collarbone.

blocks.speaker: Natasha
blocks.content: With just a little force, Natasha's neck would snap. But with her speed…
She could definitely overpower them.
Ultimately, it was because Balkan, Smodo, and Zetaro were being held hostage that they endured.

blocks.speaker: Smodo
blocks.content: This tastes awful. Maggots are better. Natasha, got any other meat?

blocks.speaker: Natasha
blocks.content: It's rotten. I'll see what I can find.
The demon whipped the chain like a whip.

blocks.speaker: Demons
blocks.content: Heh. Well, I've given you what you wanted, so come along. Today's entertainment is going to be very fun.
Feeling the blades penetrate, Natasha raised her head slowly and stepped back.

blocks.speaker: Balkan
blocks.content: Natasha, leave.
Just kill them all and leave. You can definitely do that. No need to endure because of us.
The demons' eyes widened.
The bone jail glowed red-hot like molten metal.

blocks.speaker: Smodo
blocks.content: You all... really want to die?
The jail, powered by Brigade Leader Magasura's ability, was designed to explode in response to magic energy.

blocks.speaker: Smodo
blocks.content: Shut up. I'll really kill you. Magasura won't blame us.
The one who had originally ordered to keep Gustav the Fourth alive was the 9th Legion Leader Paimon.
However, she had perished, becoming part of the demon realm.

blocks.speaker: Smodo
blocks.content: Of course, the order wasn't invalidated, but its psychological binding force was much weakened.

blocks.speaker: Balkan
blocks.content: Natasha, end it.
Knowing all this, Zetaro agreed with Balkan and Smodo.

blocks.speaker: Zetaro
blocks.content: I'm bored too. Harvitz isn't here anymore, what's the point in playing more? Let's just go back.
The demon clutching the chain gritted its teeth.

blocks.speaker: Demons
blocks.content: You...!
As the blade sank deeper into Natasha's neck, bubbles formed in the heated bone jail.
In a moment of crisis where one side could be annihilated…

blocks.speaker: Natasha
blocks.content: It's okay.
Natasha smiled at her friends.

blocks.speaker: Natasha
blocks.content: I'll go back for now. Rest up here. I'll bring fresher food next time.
There was no special reaction.
Killing intent flashed in Zetaro's eyes, and Smodo's face, chewing on the meat, became expressionless.
As Natasha walked back to the demons, the blades withdrew, and the bone jail rapidly cooled.
As she walked away, Balkan asked.

blocks.speaker: Balkan
blocks.content: What are you thinking?

blocks.speaker: Natasha
blocks.content: The same as you. I'm not thinking anything. Just surviving because I can hold on a bit longer.

blocks.speaker: Balkan
blocks.content: Is there anything you can't endure?
Natasha was like a doll.
Bitten, torn, burned, or even when her stuffing was pulled out, she still smiled.
Leaving the question unanswered, Natasha was dragged away by the demons.
Smodo threw a bone.

blocks.speaker: Smodo
blocks.content: Ah, crap. It tastes terrible. She's strange. She seems a bit different from us.

blocks.speaker: Smodo
blocks.content: Exactly.
Zetaro, shocked, tremblingly dropped the meat.

blocks.speaker: Zetaro
blocks.content: I found it. I really found something interesting. Harvitz must have wanted to feel this.
What are you talking about?

blocks.speaker: Zetaro
blocks.content: Harvitz abandoned us, right? How interesting is that? Must have been thrilling.

blocks.speaker: Smodo
blocks.content: Yeah, right. Balkan didn't expect it either. He really knows how to play.

blocks.speaker: Zetaro
blocks.content: This time, we'll play. We'll kill Harvitz. Capture him, cut off his limbs, mock him… Hehehe!
The mere thought seemed thrilling, and Zetaro knelt, spreading his arms.

blocks.speaker: Zetaro
blocks.content: In the end, I'll slowly slit his throat with a scalpel while looking him in the eye. Then Harvitz will… Harvitz will…
His eyelids fluttered.

blocks.speaker: Zetaro
blocks.content: How much fun would that be?
The ultimate ecstasy that a human could experience.

blocks.speaker: Zetaro
blocks.content: Imagine it. He wants to fight back but can't because his limbs are cut off. How funny would that be? It's a masterpiece. Surely it will make him cry his eyes out.

blocks.speaker: Smodo
blocks.content: Hmm.
Balkan finally showed interest, and a smile spread across Smodo's face.

blocks.speaker: Smodo
blocks.content: We can't end it like this. Harvitz will tell me again. He'll say this to me.
Zetaro lowered his arms, choked up.

blocks.speaker: Zetaro
blocks.content: Indeed… Zetaro is the best.

blocks.speaker: Smodo
blocks.content: Hehe, yes. Well, I'm not Harvitz, but surely the game you created is the best. This time it will really be fun.

blocks.speaker: Smodo
blocks.content: Then, we play more?
The three simultaneously looked outside the bone jail.

blocks.speaker: Zetaro
blocks.content: Harvitz, the sunset.
